texto: South Korea first allowed women into the military in 1950 during the Korean War. Back then, female soldiers mainly held administrative and support positions. Women began to take on combat roles in the 1990s when the three military academies, exclusive to men, began accepting women.
explicacao: Citação de Kim Young-ha sobre temas ligados a reflexao. “South Korea first allowed women into the military in 1950 during the Korean War. Back then, femal…” articula reflexao, women, support em registro de reflexao, com relevância para contextos como support e men.

frase_original: South Korea first allowed women into the military in 1950 during the Korean War. Back then, female soldiers mainly held administrative and support positions. Women began to take on combat roles in the 1990s when the three military academies, exclusive to men, began accepting women.

texto: In the early 2000s, people expected that anonymity on the Internet would be positive for the development of democracy in South Korea. In a Confucian culture like South Korea's, hierarchy can block the free exchange of opinions in face-to-face situations. The web offered a way around that.
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a Kim Young-ha: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de temas ligados a reflexao. O trecho “In the early 2000s, people expected that anonymity on the Internet would be positive for the deve…” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que reflexao, culture, democracy pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de mudanca e democracy.

frase_original: In the early 2000s, people expected that anonymity on the Internet would be positive for the development of democracy in South Korea. In a Confucian culture like South Korea's, hierarchy can block the free exchange of opinions in face-to-face situations. The web offered a way around that.
